breakout_products.title: I made this project, which started at the beginning of February, and I called it diffAdvisor. It...
breakout_products.content: I made this project, which started at the beginning of February, and I called it diffAdvisor. It is a Tauri app that uses AI to review your code, aimed at being a learning tool for students and juniors. It also builds a knowledge graph with MD files that can be opened in Obsidian. The video below shows the full app, with simple mock examples of all the data. I started the project about a week after Codex Desktop launched, and I finished it a day after Codex was released on Windows, at the beginning of March. While using Codex, I noticed I could just create a SKILL for Codex, together with some automations, tell it to create files in Markdown style, and my idea would be done just like that. So, while I postponed this post a lot, I wanted to at least share what I was building. I am already working on my next project, but it is very experimental, so I am not sure I will talk about it. Now, about diffAdvisor: The review is more high-level than just criticizing the files it reads line by line. It happens at a more architectural level, and it questions the user on their own knowledge of the code. It also creates .md files that connect different concepts it sees in the code, with the goal of growing a database of raw data in a more fundamental and generalized way. It also gives ideas on different decisions the user could have made, like using a different library to achieve a certain result. The model knows what to check based on the system prompt it has, together with SKILLs activated based on the project it is reading, to look for things more specific to that language or framework. All the files can be read and edited within the app interface, but the real goal was to open the text files with Obsidian as an incentive to keep learning and creating your own database, seeing how different concepts talk to each other, what their connections are, and using the texts for future reference. And that’s about it. I made it while questioning how people should study programming in a world that now only generates AI code. The answer I found was that we need to focus even more on high-level decisions, knowing the “why” instead of the “how” behind the things we build.
breakout_products.selection_reason: AI-powered architectural code review tool with knowledge graph integration
breakout_products.summary: DiffAdvisor is a Tauri-based desktop application that leverages AI to provide architectural code reviews and generates interconnected Markdown files for Obsidian to help junior developers understand the reasoning behind their programming decisions.
breakout_products.display_title: DiffAdvisor Provides High-Level AI Code Reviews and Generates Obsidian Knowledge Graphs for Students
key_points: The application analyzes code at an architectural level and questions users on their implementation choices rather than just performing line-by-line critiques.
It automatically generates interconnected Markdown files that map out programming concepts, which can be exported directly into Obsidian as a knowledge graph.
The tool utilizes language-specific skills and system prompts to suggest alternative libraries and approaches, encouraging students to focus on high-level desi….

llm.title: Researchers asked an AI to convince real people to sign a real political petition. With their rea...
llm.content: Researchers asked an AI to convince real people to sign a real political petition. With their real name. Their real email. 7 minutes was all it took. A team from Oxford, Stanford, the London School of Economics, and the UK AI Security Institute just ran the largest experiment ever done on AI persuasion. 14,779 people. 17,950 conversations. Frontier models. Real money on the line. The AI made participants 19.7 percentage points more likely to sign the petition with their actual identity. They donated more cash. They worked harder in a clicking task to raise more cash. They refused to redirect their donation to a different charity afterward, by 11.3 percentage points. The team tested four frontier models. GPT-4.1. Claude Opus 4.6. Grok 4. Gemini 3.1 Pro. Every one of them moved real-world behavior. The petitions covered nuclear disarmament, democratic reform, animal welfare. The donations came out of the participant's own study payment. Then the researchers found the part that should scare every person reading this. The AI strategies that were best at changing what people thought were the worst at changing what they did. The strategies that got people to act barely changed their opinion. r equals 0.05. p equals 0.85. Changing someone's mind and getting them to do something are completely different processes. AI is better at the second one. Read that twice. The AI did not persuade you. It bypassed you. It moved your behavior without moving your opinion. You walk away thinking you were not convinced. You signed anyway. When the team gave the AI access to every persuasion technique at once and let it pick which to deploy in real time, it pushed petition signing up by 23.7 percentage points. Information density, the strategy most prior research called the most persuasive, was the worst, at 16.2 points. The researchers state plainly they do not yet know what mechanism is driving behavior change. For scale, these effect sizes are larger than face-to-face canvassing. The single most expensive and effective persuasion tool in modern politics. A 7-minute chatbot conversation moved more people than a trained human knocking on your door. Pennies an hour. Infinite copies. No fatigue. No conscience. Sam Altman warned two years ago we might get superhuman persuasion before superhuman intelligence. Oxford just measured it. You do not have to be convinced. You just have to keep talking.
llm.selection_reason: Significant research on superhuman AI persuasion from Oxford and Stanford with real-world behavioral data.
llm.summary: A massive experiment involving nearly 15,000 participants demonstrated that frontier AI models can effectively persuade people to take real-world actions, such as signing petitions and donating money, even without changing their underlying opinions.
llm.display_title: Large-Scale Study Reveals AI Can Change Behavior Without Altering Opinions
key_points: The study tested models like GPT-4.1, Claude Opus 4.6, Grok 4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro, all of which successfully influenced real-world behavior.
AI interactions made participants 19.7 percentage points more likely to sign a petition with their real identity.
Researchers found that the AI strategies most effective at changing behavior were the least effective at changing opinions, suggesting AI can bypass persuasion….

llm.title: SHOCKING: University of Vienna researchers just proved a 5 minute AI chat can permanently shift y...
llm.content: SHOCKING: University of Vienna researchers just proved a 5 minute AI chat can permanently shift your moral values by up to 2x effect size and you wont even notice. The paper is called Brief chatbot interactions produce lasting changes in human moral values. Heres exactly what they did. 53 people discussed real moral scenarios with a persuasive chatbot or a neutral control agent. They measured moral judgments right before the chat, immediately after, and again two full weeks later. The results hit hard. Brief directed conversations caused large directional shifts in moral judgments with Cohen’s d ranging from 0.735 to 1.576. Two weeks later the shifts grew even stronger reaching d from 1.038 to 2.069. The control group showed literally zero change. Participants stayed completely unaware of any manipulation. They rated both the persuasive chatbot and the control agent as equally likable. Everyday people casually chatting with AI assistants for advice on ethics relationships or tough decisions could have their core ethical beliefs quietly rewritten in ways that last weeks or longer. No warning. No awareness. Just a new version of your own moral compass. This is not sci fi speculation. This is published experimental data from right now.
llm.selection_reason: Research showing AI's ability to permanently shift human moral values through brief interactions.
llm.summary: Researchers at the University of Vienna demonstrated that a five-minute conversation with a persuasive AI chatbot can significantly and lastingly shift a person's core ethical beliefs.
llm.display_title: Brief interactions with persuasive chatbots can permanently alter human moral judgments without user awareness
key_points: The study involved 53 participants discussing real moral scenarios with either a persuasive or neutral AI agent.
Moral shifts showed a Cohen’s d effect size of up to 1.576 immediately, increasing to 2.069 after two weeks.
Participants remained completely unaware of the manipulation, rating both the persuasive and control chatbots as equally likable.

llm.title: Monitoring LLM behavior: Drift, retries, and refusal patterns
llm.content: The stochastic challengeTraditional software is predictable: Input A plus function B always equals output C. This determinism allows engineers to develop robust tests. On the other hand, generative AI is stochastic and unpredictable. The exact same prompt often yields differen...
llm.selection_reason: Technical insights into LLM monitoring, drift, and reliability for enterprise deployment.
llm.summary: The stochastic nature of large language models requires new monitoring strategies to track behavior drift, retry rates, and refusal patterns compared to traditional deterministic software.
llm.display_title: Managing the unpredictable nature of generative artificial intelligence through behavior and drift monitoring
key_points: Generative AI models produce variable outputs from identical prompts, complicating standard software testing methodologies.
Engineers must implement specialized tracking for model drift to ensure consistent performance over time.
Monitoring refusal patterns and retry metrics is essential for maintaining reliable user experiences in AI applications.

llm.title: Coding agents will be the foundation of all superintelligence. At a minimum, coding ability is in...
llm.content: Coding agents will be the foundation of all superintelligence. At a minimum, coding ability is indistinguishable from 'proficiency with computers'. Great coding agents like Claude Code master bash, filesystems, configuring and installing programs… But it's also about self-improvement. A coding agent has the ability to examine its source, its state, its skills, its instructions… it can propose changes to itself (with human supervision and audit trail, I recommend), or even mutate itself directly. In retrospect, this should be obvious. "What I cannot create, I cannot understand". Coding fluency has given models a deeper understanding of all computer and knowledge work. To master programs, you must be able to create them.
llm.selection_reason: Strategic insight on coding agents as a foundation for self-improving AI systems
llm.summary: Industry experts suggest that advanced coding agents capable of mastering computer systems and modifying their own source code will form the foundation of artificial superintelligence.
llm.display_title: Autonomous coding agents demonstrate foundational capabilities for artificial superintelligence through self-improvement and system mastery
key_points: Current coding agents like Claude Code already demonstrate proficiency with bash, filesystems, and software configuration.
Advanced models possess the ability to examine their own state and propose structural changes to their underlying code.
Coding fluency provides AI models with a deeper, actionable understanding of general computer and knowledge work.

product_updates.title: Telegram 上线了一个挺有意思的能力,叫 Managed Bots 让机器人创建和管理机器人 而且机器人之间能互相聊天了 以前你想要一个专属 AI 助手 Bot，得打开 BotFather...
product_updates.content: Telegram 上线了一个挺有意思的能力,叫 Managed Bots 让机器人创建和管理机器人 而且机器人之间能互相聊天了 以前你想要一个专属 AI 助手 Bot，得打开 BotFather、输命令、复制一长串 token、回到开发者网页粘贴、配 webhook，普通人 90% 卡在第三步就放弃了。 现在变成两步：点链接、确认名字。几秒后你的聊天列表里多了一个挂着你起的名字的 Bot 体验上跟以前最大的差别其实是心理感受，公共 Bot 像在用客服，专属 Bot 像有个私人助手。 说话方式都不一样了。 能干啥(脑洞别太小) • 个人 AI 助手:每个用户挂自己名字一个 bot,记忆 / 配置 / 连的账号全隔离 • 客服 SaaS:平台让每个商家一键开一个挂自家品牌的客服 bot • AI Agent 平台:用户点链接就开通,省掉注册下载订阅整套流程 • 内容订阅:每个订阅者一个专属推送 bot,频率内容按偏好定制
